content.problemStatement:
Chef is participating in an election going up against his enemy, AntiChef.

There were NNN voters, and their votes are described by a binary string SSS where Si=1S_i = 1Si​=1 if and only if the iii-th voter voted for Chef.

The votes will be counted one by one, in order from 111 to NNN. At any point, Chef can say to stop the count, and the remaining votes will not be counted.

Chef wants to win. How many ways are there for Chef to win depending on when he says to stop the count? 222 ways are said to be distinct if there is some voter whose vote got counted in the first way, but not in the second (or vice versa).

Note that a tie is not a win for Chef, and Chef can also say to stop the count after all NNN votes have been counted.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains a binary string SSS.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of ways for Chef to say to stop the count such that he wins.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : Chef can say to stop the count after the first voter is counted, or after the second voter, or after the third voter.
Test Case 2 : No matter when Chef says to stop the count, he loses.
